notable_pick_trades: Traded 4th and 5th rounders to Saints for Rashid Shaheed at 2025 deadline. Original 6th went to JAX in 2024. Only have picks 32, 64, 96, and CLE's 6th-rounder.
roster_context: Reigning Super Bowl LX champions picking last in every round. Core offense and defense largely intact under contract, but facing significant free agent attrition at CB, EDGE, RB, and WR. OC Klint Kubiak left for Raiders HC job; replaced by Brian Fleury. Only 4 draft picks severely limits ability to address multiple needs. Cap space (~$58-60M) is ample but must fund extensions for Jaxon Smith-Njigba and Devon Witherspoon. Team philosophy is to keep the championship roster intact rather than make splashy acquisitions.

needs.explanation: Super Bowl MVP Kenneth Walker III was not franchise tagged and is expected to leave in free agency as multiple sources indicate he'll get offers above Seattle's comfort level ($12-13M/yr). Meanwhile, Zach Charbonnet (torn ACL in playoffs) is expected to miss a significant chunk of 2026. Kenny McIntosh also missed all of 2025 with an ACL injury. The backfield currently consists of George Holani (ERFA) and Velus Jones Jr. (futures deal). For a run-first offense, this is an urgent hole.
needs.depth: Zach Charbonnet (ACL recovery, may miss early 2026), George Holani (ERFA), Kenny McIntosh (ACL recovery), Velus Jones Jr.

needs.explanation: Both starting outside corners Riq Woolen and Josh Jobe are UFAs. Woolen reportedly was not viewed as a fit for Macdonald's defense and is projected at $19M/yr — likely too expensive. Jobe is the most likely to return at ~$9-10M/yr but still might leave. Behind Devon Witherspoon (elite CB1), the cupboard is essentially bare. Nick Emmanwori can play nickel/slot but is primarily a safety. Nehemiah Pritchett is the only other CB under contract and was a rotational piece. Losing both starters would be catastrophic for the defense.
needs.current_starter: Devon Witherspoon (CB1, under contract), Riq Woolen (UFA), Josh Jobe (UFA)
needs.depth: Nehemiah Pritchett, Nick Emmanwori (slot capable). Extremely thin if Woolen and Jobe both depart.

needs.explanation: The Seahawks' dominant four-man edge rotation is in danger of being gutted. DeMarcus Lawrence (turning 34) is actively contemplating retirement after winning the Super Bowl. Boye Mafe is a UFA expected to leave after being benched at times and projected at $18M/yr. Uchenna Nwosu has a bloated $20M cap hit and has battled injuries — could be a cap casualty. That would leave only Derick Hall (contract year) as a proven contributor. The team went from elite EDGE depth to potential crisis in one offseason.
needs.current_starter: DeMarcus Lawrence (considering retirement), Uchenna Nwosu ($20M cap hit, injury concerns)
needs.depth: Derick Hall (contract year 2026), Connor O'Toole, Jared Ivey — significant drop-off after Hall

needs.explanation: Coby Bryant (converted from CB to S, 4 INTs in 2025) is a UFA with significant outside interest. Julian Love is the other starting safety and is under contract. Nick Emmanwori was a standout rookie in a hybrid S/nickel role. If Bryant leaves, the Seahawks still have Love and Emmanwori, but lose a ball-hawking playmaker. Ty Okada (ERFA, tendered) provides depth. Need is moderate given Emmanwori's versatility.
needs.depth: Nick Emmanwori (hybrid S/nickel, under contract), Ty Okada (ERFA tendered), D'Anthony Bell (signed 2025)

needs.explanation: Leonard Williams and Byron Murphy II anchor one of the NFL's best interior DL duos and both are under contract. However, the defense has been asking Murphy to play nose tackle too often when he's better as a 3-tech. There's a need for a true nose tackle to play alongside Murphy. Jarran Reed is aging and Brandon Pili is an RFA. Rylie Mills showed promise as a rookie but the room could use another run-stuffing body.
needs.depth: Jarran Reed (veteran), Rylie Mills (2nd year), Brandon Pili (RFA)

needs.explanation: No movement at OT. Charles Cross and Abe Lucas are entrenched starters. Josh Jones departed but was depth only. Seahawks have younger interior/tackle developmental options. Still a position to watch for depth in the draft but tier unchanged.

While first-round pick Grey Zabel solidified LG, center Jalen Sundell is an UDFA who performed admirably but may not be the long-term answer. Right guard Anthony Bradford is the weakest link on the line — inconsistent and had a below-average PFF grade for much of the season. Multiple analysts have identified center/guard as the key remaining OL weakness. The 2026 draft class has quality center prospects that could fit Seattle's zone-blocking scheme.
needs.current_starter: Grey Zabel (LG), Jalen Sundell (C), Anthony Bradford (RG)
needs.depth: Olu Oluwatimi (C backup), Christian Haynes (G/C), Mason Richman (G/T swing)

needs.explanation: Both starting LBs retained: Drake Thomas (2yr/$8M extension) and Ernest Jones IV (already under contract). Thomas had 96 tackles and 3.5 sacks as a first-year starter in 2025. LB is fully addressed. Tier 4 — no meaningful draft investment needed here.
Sam Darnold is the unquestioned starter under a multi-year deal. Drew Lock returns as the backup on a 2yr/$5M deal. QB room is stable. No change needed.

Rashid Shaheed is not close to an extension and is expected to test free agency, with multiple teams interested. If he leaves, the WR room behind Jaxon Smith-Njigba is thin. Cooper Kupp (turning 33) is returning but is aging and had limited production. Second-year Tory Horton is recovering from a shin injury. Jake Bobo was not tendered as an RFA. The team needs a complementary speed/vertical threat opposite JSN.
needs.current_starter: Jaxon Smith-Njigba (WR1, extension pending), Cooper Kupp (WR2, age 33)
needs.depth: Tory Horton (2nd year, recovering from injury), Steven Sims, River Cracraft — very thin if Shaheed departs

free_agency_impact: Kenneth Walker III (RB) → Kansas City Chiefs
Signed Rashid Shaheed (WR) from Seattle Seahawks (re-sign; acquired via trade from Saints in Nov 2025) — $17.0M AAV
Signed Josh Jobe (CB) from Seattle Seahawks (re-sign) — $8.0M AAV
Signed Drake Thomas (LB) from Seattle Seahawks (re-sign; was RFA) — $4.0M AAV
Signed Emanuel Wilson (RB) from Green Bay Packers — $2.1M AAV
Signed Rodney Thomas II (S) from Indianapolis Colts — terms TBD AAV
Signed D'Anthony Bell (S) from Carolina Panthers (was SEA, waived, claimed by CAR late 2025) — terms TBD AAV
Signed AJ Finley (S) from Seattle Seahawks (re-sign) — terms TBD AAV
Signed Brandon Pili (IDL) from Seattle Seahawks (re-sign) — $2.0M AAV
Signed Brady Russell (TE) from Seattle Seahawks (re-sign; was RFA) — terms TBD AAV
Signed Josh Jones (IOL) from Seattle Seahawks (re-sign) — $4.0M AAV
Signed Jake Bobo (WR) from Seattle Seahawks (ROFR tender) — $3.5M AAV
Signed Eric Saubert (TE) from Seattle Seahawks (extension signed Dec 2025 while on roster; signed originally March 2025) — terms TBD AAV
scheme_fit_notes: Mike Macdonald runs an aggressive, multiple-front defense that relies on a deep rotation of EDGE rushers and versatile defensive backs who can disguise coverages. The defense frequently deploys 5+ DBs with Emmanwori as a hybrid safety/nickel. CBs must be physical and comfortable in man coverage. EDGE prospects with pass-rush versatility and a motor are valued over pure speed rushers. On offense, the Seahawks run a wide-zone blocking scheme under new OC Brian Fleury (similar to Kubiak's system). IOL prospects who excel in zone blocking with lateral agility are ideal fits. The offense is run-first, making the RB vacancy especially impactful.
